title: Psephellus arpensis
intro: Psephellus arpensis, also known as Mountain Asters, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, contributing to its potential health benefits in treating various conditions such as wounds, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Psephellus arpensis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Psephellus arpensis is classified within the family Asteraceae. This classification places it within the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. Further, Psephellus arpensis is also classified as a member of the order Asterales. Within the family Asteraceae, it is part of the subfamily Carduoideae. More specifically, it belongs to the tribe Cardueae.
